description: USB pairing crypto conformance vectors (X25519 / SHA-256 / HKDF-SHA256 / HMAC-SHA256) for R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 (USB Pairing, Wired, MITM-Protected). Canonical home is R2-PROVISION §5.3.4. Inputs are deterministic test values; keys are TEST-ONLY. ACTIVE SET (SIMPLE SECURE PAIRING — R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.47, Roy explicit 2026-07-12): §5.3.4 is now a single simple-secure spec (first-pair MITM security kept byte-exact; the generation/rotation/durable-journal/crash-recovery machinery REMOVED — full durability is a parked field track). The REQUIRED (active) vectors are UP1-UP8 (X25519/commitment/SAS/link_key), UP13 (usb_link_id), UP14 (non-contributory-Z reject + choreography + the three terminal key-confirmation MACs confirm/done/ack), and UP18 (the SIMPLE same-key reconnect tag = HMAC-SHA256(link_key,'r2-usb-reconnect-v1'||nonce_rc||usb_link_id)[..16] = 2f62edaa...; usb_link_id = the CAPS hive_id_bytes, stable/TG-independent per R2-USB §3.6). UP1-UP8/UP13 were GENERATED from r2-hive's canonical usb_pair helpers + independently verified byte-exact by specs (2026-06-09); UP14 terminal MACs are hive-CONFIRMED + byte-exact; UP18 is Python-reverified byte-exact and reproduces the r2-hive original gen-free reconnect helper. NON-ACTIVE / HISTORICAL (active:false — the retired generation design, kept for the parked full-durability field track only, NOT required): UP9-UP12 (generation-BOUND reconnect tag), UP15 (two-generation grace + accept_mac), UP16 (host_gens-tamper reject), UP17 (compromised-K0 activation-reject). A conforming implementation reproduces every ACTIVE vector exactly and does NOT need the active:false ones. Do not hand-edit the r2-hive-generated base entries.

vectors.description: eph public key from host secret
eph public key from peripheral secret
ECDH shared secret Z (agrees both sides)

vectors.status_note: v0.40 GENERATION-BOUND reconnect tag (supersedes the pre-v0.39 gen-free construction, old value 2f62edaa... REMOVED). tag = HMAC-SHA256(K[gen], 'r2-usb-reconnect-v1' || nonce_rc[16] || hive_id[16] || u32be(gen) || genlist(host_gens))[..16]; genlist = concat(u32be(g) for g in sorted-unique host_gens). PROVISIONAL pending hive usb_pair.rs impl of the gen-based reconnect.
vectors.description: reconnect tag, first-pair single generation (gen=1, host_gens=[1])
verify_reconnect_tag rejects wrong link key (gen=1, host_gens=[1])

two-generation grace: gen=1 chosen from host_gens=[0,1] (K1-preferring); tag + accept_mac both bind genlist([0,1]). accept_mac = HMAC-SHA256(K[1], 'r2-usb-reconnect-accept-v1' || nonce_rc || hive_id || u32be(1) || genlist([0,1]))[..16].

host_gens-TAMPER negative KAT (anti-downgrade, android-codex HIGH; v0.41 CORRECTED to isolate the binding). The host SENDS host_gens=[0,1]. A MITM strips gen 1, so the peripheral RECEIVES [0], legitimately selects gen=0, and answers tag over genlist([0]) at gen=0 = f93e3a82704532cc8f9d8457784094de. The host then verifies the response at the SAME gen=0 and SAME key K[0], but over the EXACT list it SENT [0,1] = deb8dcb28f837f16f14afc3499229ca4. They differ ONLY because of the host_gens binding (gen and key are identical) => host MUST reject (reconnect_failed). This cleanly isolates the offered-list authentication (the earlier draft changed gen+key+list at once and did not test the binding).
vectors.note: same gen=0, same key K[0]; tags differ solely due to genlist([0]) vs genlist([0,1]) => downgrade detected

vectors.description: compromised-K0 activation-reject KAT (net generation invariant, supervisor+android-codex). Host is PROMOTED (holds K1=gen 1). A holder of ONLY K0 (gen 0) presents a VALID gen-0 reconnect tag. The tag verifies cryptographically, but because gen 0 < promoted gen 1, the host MUST NOT bring up a live data session: RECOVERY-ONLY (re-rotation with fresh operator SAS). A compromised K0 therefore cannot obtain a live K1 session (it cannot pass the operator SAS). Asserts activation is generation-gated, not just retirement.

vectors.status_note: ACTIVE / REQUIRED (R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.47 — SIMPLE SECURE PAIRING, Roy explicit 2026-07-12). This gen-free same-key reconnect tag is the single REQUIRED reconnect construction; it reproduces the original r2-hive gen-free reconnect helper and is Python-reverified byte-exact. The generation-bound reconnect (UP9-UP12/UP15-UP17) is retired to the parked full-durability field track (active:false).
vectors.description: REQUIRED simple same-key reconnect tag = HMAC-SHA256(link_key, 'r2-usb-reconnect-v1' || nonce_rc[16] || usb_link_id[16])[..16] (usb_link_id = the CAPS hive_id_bytes; the test placeholder value is unchanged, byte-exact). The single reconnect construction of the simple-secure spec (R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.47); reproduces the r2-hive original helper.
vectors.usb_link_id_note: The bound identity is the CAPS hive_id_bytes = usb_link_id (stable, TG-independent, R2-USB §3.6) — NOT the mesh hive_id. The field name 'hive_id_bytes' here is the CAPS field name; its value is a test placeholder for usb_link_id. R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.46 names the KDF input usb_link_id.

vectors.description: USB-link identity (CAPS hive_id_bytes) — stable, TG-independent: usb_link_id = HKDF-SHA256(salt='r2-usb-link-v1', ikm=device_master_secret, info='')[0:16]. R2-USB §3.6 / R2-WIRE §6.2.1.

R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.47 SIMPLE SECURE host-opens pairing + THREE-MESSAGE key-confirmation terminal (CONFIRM->DONE->PAIR_ACK, link_key-authenticated mutual key-confirmation — no journal/generations). Choreography frames HELLO/COMMIT/HOST_REVEAL/REVEAL byte-locked; the three terminal MACs (confirm/done/ack) byte-exact + hive-confirmed. Order: PAIR_HELLO_HOST(4,empty) -> PAIR_COMMIT(5) -> PAIR_HOST_REVEAL(13) -> PAIR_REVEAL(6) -> PAIR_CONFIRM(7) -> PAIR_DONE(8) -> PAIR_ACK(14). Simple reconnect tag = UP18.

sequence.name: PAIR_ACK
sequence.note: key-confirmation finalizer; peripheral persists the link_key on verify

abort_bad_key.name: PAIR_ABORT
abort_bad_key.note: sent when Z is non-contributory (all-zero); SAS MUST NOT be displayed

protocol_error_premature_host_reveal.when: PAIR_HOST_REVEAL(13) or host eph_pk/nonce before PAIR_COMMIT

non_contributory_cases.note: u=1 low-order point (RFC 7748 §6.1) — X25519 yields all-zero; expect reject

vectors.status_note: v0.47 SIMPLE SECURE PAIRING: all THREE terminal key-confirmation MACs (confirm/done/ack) byte-exact + hive-confirmed. transcript = eph_pk_host||eph_pk_peripheral||nonce_host||nonce_peripheral (128B under the link_key K), labels confirm-host-v1/confirm-peripheral-v1/ack-host-v1, 16B trunc: confirm=4e4c5ff2..., done=08ba274f..., ack=1ec03c3d... These three form a plain mutual key-confirmation (no journal/generations/phases). The reconnect is the simple same-key tag UP18. NO crash-durability/journal/rotation KATs are owed (that machinery is parked). NOTE (v0.47): the host persists K at PAIR_DONE-verify (before PAIR_ACK), so a lost PAIR_ACK / peripheral-crash-pre-persist yields a durable ONE-SIDED-key state (H=new, P=old) — POSSIBLE — recoverable, no key compromise, but an availability/ceremony DoS an attacker CAN force (no deadlock, no reconciliation; the next reconnect fails then operator re-pair; operator re-pair overrides the known-key reconnect path). This is a behavioral outcome, not a byte-KAT.

negative_premature_host_reveal.description: PAIR_HOST_REVEAL(13) arriving BEFORE PAIR_COMMIT(5) MUST be rejected with protocol_error (enforces commit-before-reveal). Also a host eph_pk/nonce before COMMIT.

vectors.owed_state_crash_vectors: NONE (byte-KATs). Crash/journal/rotation KATs are OUT of scope for the simple-secure spec (R2-PROVISION §5.3.4 v0.47). Recovery = re-pair on any doubt. There is no durable pending key or generation grace, so no split-brain RECONCILIATION protocol exists — BUT a durable one-sided-key state (H=new/P=old) IS possible after a lost PAIR_ACK (host persists K before ACK); it is recoverable (no key compromise) + operator-recoverable via re-pair (behavioral trace, not a byte-KAT). Crash-durability KATs belong to the parked full-durability field track only.
